rs3892097.description: CYP2D6*4 — loss-of-function (splicing defect, most common null allele)

phenotype_to_guidance.Normal Metabolizer: Standard codeine dosing expected to provide typical analgesia via CYP2D6-mediated conversion to morphine.
phenotype_to_guidance.Intermediate Metabolizer: Reduced morphine formation expected. Codeine may provide inadequate pain relief. Consider alternative analgesics if pain control is insufficient.
phenotype_to_guidance.Poor Metabolizer: Minimal to no morphine formation. Codeine will likely be ineffective for pain relief. CPIC recommends using an alternative analgesic NOT metabolized by CYP2D6.
phenotype_to_guidance.Ultra-rapid Metabolizer: Significantly increased morphine formation. AVOID codeine due to risk of life-threatening respiratory depression. Use alternative analgesics.
